title: देवी खड्गमाला स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री देवी खड्गमाला स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीशुद्धशक्तिमालामहामन्त्रस्य उपस्थेन्द्रियाधिष्ठायी
वरुणादित्यरुद्राणामृषीणां त्रिष्टुप् छन्दः।
श्रीमहात्रिपुरसुन्दरी देवता।
दैवतोपरि विनियोगः॥

ॐ ह्रीं श्रीं -

हृदयदेवी, शिरोदेवी, शिखादेवी, कवचदेवी,
नेत्रदेवी, अस्त्रदेवी।

कामेश्वरी, भगमालिनी, नित्यक्लिन्ने, भेरुण्डा,
वह्निवासिनी, महावज्रेश्वरी, शिवदूती, त्वरिता,
कुलसुन्दरी, नित्या, नीलपताका, विजया,
सर्वमंगला, ज्वालामालिनी, चित्रा।

॥ प्रथमावरण - त्रैलोक्यमोहन चक्रम् ॥

अणिमासिद्धे, लघिमासिद्धे, गरिमासिद्धे, महिमासिद्धे,
ईशित्वसिद्धे, वशित्वसिद्धे, प्राकाम्यसिद्धे, भुक्तिसिद्धे,
इच्छासिद्धे, प्राप्तिसिद्धे।

ब्राह्मी, माहेश्वरी, कौमारी, वैष्णवी,
वाराही, माहेन्द्री, चामुण्डा, महालक्ष्मी।

सर्वसंक्षोभिणी, सर्वविद्राविणी, सर्वाकर्षिणी,
सर्ववशंकरी, सर्वोन्मादिनी, सर्वमहांकुशा,
सर्वखेचरी, सर्वबीजे, सर्वयोने, सर्वत्रिखण्डा।

॥ द्वितीयावरण - सर्वाशापरिपूरक चक्रम् ॥

कामाकर्षिणी, बुद्ध्याकर्षिणी, अहंकाराकर्षिणी,
शब्दाकर्षिणी, स्पर्शाकर्षिणी, रूपाकर्षिणी,
रसाकर्षिणी, गन्धाकर्षिणी, चित्ताकर्षिणी,
धैर्याकर्षिणी, स्मृत्याकर्षिणी, नामाकर्षिणी,
बीजाकर्षिणी, आत्माकर्षिणी, अमृताकर्षिणी,
शरीराकर्षिणी।

॥ तृतीयावरण - सर्वसंक्षोभण चक्रम् ॥

अनङ्गकुसुमा, अनङ्गमेखला, अनङ्गमदना,
अनङ्गमदनातुरा, अनङ्गरेखा, अनङ्गवेगिनी,
अनङ्गांकुशा, अनङ्गमालिनी।

॥ चतुर्थावरण - सर्वसौभाग्यदायक चक्रम् ॥

सर्वसंक्षोभिणी, सर्वविद्राविणी, सर्वाकर्षिणी,
सर्वाह्लादिनी, सर्वसम्मोहिनी, सर्वस्तम्भिनी,
सर्वजृम्भिणी, सर्ववशंकरी, सर्वरञ्जिनी,
सर्वोन्मादिनी, सर्वार्थसाधिनी, सर्वसम्पत्तिपूरिणी,
सर्वमन्त्रमयी, सर्वद्वन्द्वक्षयंकरी।

॥ पञ्चमावरण - सर्वार्थसाधक चक्रम् ॥

सर्वसिद्धिप्रदा, सर्वसम्पत्प्रदा, सर्वप्रियंकरी,
सर्वमंगलकारिणी, सर्वकामप्रदा, सर्वदुःखविमोचिनी,
सर्वमृत्युप्रशमनी, सर्वविघ्ननिवारिणी,
सर्वांगसुन्दरी, सर्वसौभाग्यदायिनी।

॥ षष्ठावरण - सर्वरक्षाकर चक्रम् ॥

सर्वज्ञे, सर्वशक्ते, सर्वैश्वर्यप्रदायिनी,
सर्वज्ञानमयी, सर्वव्याधिविनाशिनी,
सर्वाधारस्वरूपा, सर्वपापहरा, सर्वानन्दमयी,
सर्वरक्षास्वरूपिणी, सर्वेप्सितफलप्रदा।

॥ सप्तमावरण - सर्वरोगहर चक्रम् ॥

वशिनी, कामेश्वरी, मोदिनी, विमला, अरुणा,
जयिनी, सर्वेश्वरी, कौलिनी।

॥ अष्टमावरण - सर्वसिद्धिप्रद चक्रम् ॥

बाणिनी, चापिनी, पाशिनी, अंकुशिनी।

॥ नवमावरण - सर्वानन्दमय चक्रम् ॥

महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी, महाकामेश्वरी,
महावज्रेश्वरी, महाभगमालिनी।

श्रीश्रीमहाभट्टारिका सर्वानन्दमयचक्रस्वामिनी
परापररहस्ययोगिनी श्रीमत्त्रिपुरसुन्दरी
महामहाराज्ञी नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमस्तस्यै नमो नमः॥

इदं खड्गमालामहास्तोत्ररत्नं यः पठेत् सततं सिद्धिकामः।
सोऽप्यखिलां श्रियमाप्नोत्यनन्तां मोक्षमन्ते च लभेत् परम्॥

॥ इति श्री देवी खड्गमाला स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Devi Khadgamala Stotram ||

Om Asya Shri Shuddha-shakti-mala-mahamantrasya Upasthendriya-adhishthhayi
Varunaditya-rudranam Rishinam Trishtup Chhandah.
Shri Maha-tripura-sundari Devata.
Daivatopari Viniyogah.

Om Hrim Shrim -

Hridaya-devi, Shiro-devi, Shikha-devi, Kavacha-devi,
Netra-devi, Astra-devi.

Kameshvari, Bhaga-malini, Nitya-klinne, Bherunda,
Vahni-vasini, Maha-vajreshvari, Shiva-duti, Tvarita,
Kula-sundari, Nitya, Nila-pataka, Vijaya,
Sarva-mangala, Jvala-malini, Chitra.

|| Prathamavarana - Trailokya-mohana Chakram ||

Anima-siddhe, Laghima-siddhe, Garima-siddhe, Mahima-siddhe,
Ishitva-siddhe, Vashitva-siddhe, Prakamya-siddhe, Bhukti-siddhe,
Ichha-siddhe, Prapti-siddhe.

Brahmi, Maheshvari, Kaumari, Vaishnavi,
Varahi, Mahendri, Chamunda, Mahalakshmi.

Sarva-samkshobhini, Sarva-vidravini, Sarva-akarshini,
Sarva-vashamkari, Sarvon-madini, Sarva-mahankusha,
Sarva-khechari, Sarva-bije, Sarva-yone, Sarva-trikhanda.

|| Dvitiyavarana - Sarvasha-paripuraka Chakram ||

Kama-akarshini, Buddhy-akarshini, Ahankara-akarshini,
Shabda-akarshini, Sparsha-akarshini, Rupa-akarshini,
Rasa-akarshini, Gandha-akarshini, Chitta-akarshini,
Dhairya-akarshini, Smrity-akarshini, Nama-akarshini,
Bija-akarshini, Atma-akarshini, Amrita-akarshini,
Sharira-akarshini.

|| Tritiyavarana - Sarva-samkshobhana Chakram ||

Ananga-kusuma, Ananga-mekhala, Ananga-madana,
Ananga-madanatura, Ananga-rekha, Ananga-vegini,
Ananga-ankusha, Ananga-malini.

|| Chaturthavarana - Sarva-saubhagya-dayaka Chakram ||

Sarva-samkshobhini, Sarva-vidravini, Sarva-akarshini,
Sarva-ahladini, Sarva-sammohini, Sarva-stambhini,
Sarva-jrimbhini, Sarva-vashamkari, Sarva-ranjini,
Sarvon-madini, Sarvartha-sadhini, Sarva-sampatti-purini,
Sarva-mantra-mayi, Sarva-dvandva-kshayamkari.

|| Panchamavarana - Sarvartha-sadhaka Chakram ||

Sarva-siddhi-prada, Sarva-sampat-prada, Sarva-priyamkari,
Sarva-mangala-karini, Sarva-kama-prada, Sarva-duhkha-vimochini,
Sarva-mrityu-prashamani, Sarva-vighna-nivarini,
Sarvanga-sundari, Sarva-saubhagya-dayini.

|| Shashthavarana - Sarva-rakshakara Chakram ||

Sarvajne, Sarva-shakte, Sarvaishvarya-pradayini,
Sarva-jnana-mayi, Sarva-vyadhi-vinashini,
Sarvadhara-svarupa, Sarva-papa-hara, Sarvananda-mayi,
Sarva-raksha-svarupini, Sarvepsita-phala-prada.

|| Saptamavarana - Sarva-rogahara Chakram ||

Vashini, Kameshvari, Modini, Vimala, Aruna,
Jayini, Sarveshvari, Kaulini.

|| Ashtamavarana - Sarva-siddhi-prada Chakram ||

Banini, Chapini, Pashini, Ankushini.

|| Navamavarana - Sarvananda-maya Chakram ||

Maha-tripura-sundari, Maha-kameshvari,
Maha-vajreshvari, Maha-bhagamalini.

Shri Shri Maha-bhattarika Sarvananda-maya-chakra-svamini
Para-apara-rahasya-yogini Shrimat-tripura-sundari
Maha-maharajni Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namas-tasyai Namo Namah.

Idam Khadgamala-maha-stotra-ratnam Yah Pathet Satatam Siddhi-kamah.
So'py Akhilam Shriyam Apnoty Anantam Moksham Ante Cha Labhet Param.

|| Iti Shri Devi Khadgamala Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
यह स्तोत्र श्री विद्या परम्परा का सर्वोच्च स्तोत्र है जिसमें श्रीचक्र के नौ आवरणों की सम्पूर्ण देवियों का नामोच्चारण किया जाता है।

प्रथम आवरण (त्रैलोक्यमोहन चक्र) में दस सिद्धियाँ, आठ मातृकाएँ और दस मुद्रा शक्तियाँ हैं। द्वितीय आवरण (सर्वाशापरिपूरक चक्र) में सोलह आकर्षिणी शक्तियाँ हैं जो काम, बुद्धि, अहंकार, पाँच तन्मात्रा आदि को आकर्षित करती हैं।

तृतीय आवरण में आठ अनंग शक्तियाँ, चतुर्थ में चौदह शक्तियाँ, पञ्चम में दस शक्तियाँ, षष्ठ में दस शक्तियाँ, सप्तम में आठ वागदेवियाँ, अष्टम में चार आयुध देवियाँ हैं।

नवम आवरण सर्वानन्दमय चक्र है जहाँ स्वयं श्री महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी विराजमान हैं। जो सिद्धि का इच्छुक इस खड्गमाला स्तोत्र का निरन्तर पाठ करता है, वह अनन्त श्री और अन्त में परम मोक्ष प्राप्त करता है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीशुद्धशक्तिमालामहामन्त्रस्य उपस्थेन्द्रियाधिष्ठायी वरुणादित्यरुद्राणामृषीणां त्रिष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीमहात्रिपुरसुन्दरी देवता। ॐ ह्रीं श्रीं बीजम्। श्रीत्रिपुरसुन्दरीप्रीत्यर्थे खड्गमालापाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Equivalent to performing a complete Sri Chakra puja through recitation alone
Invokes all deities of the nine avaranas (enclosures) of Sri Chakra
Invokes all eight siddhis and ten additional powers (dashamahasiddhi)
Invokes the blessing of infinite prosperity (ananta shri) and ultimate liberation (moksha)
Most powerful stotram in Sri Vidya tradition for rapid spiritual progress
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of obstacles and fulfills all desires through the sword-garland of devi
description: Devi Khadgamala Stotram — देवी खड्गमाला स्तोत्रम् — is a devotional hymn of praise offered to Goddess Durga. This revered text originates from Sri Vidya Tantra Tradition (Vamakeshvara Tantra). Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to grant all eight siddhis and ten additional powers (dashamahasiddhi) as well as equivalent to performing a complete Sri Chakra puja through recitation alone. Additionally, it is known to remove all obstacles and fulfill all desires through the sword-garland of Devi. This prayer is traditionally recited on Friday (Shukravar) and Tuesday (Mangalvar) and during Navratri and Diwali to invoke divine grace. The text contains 9 verses and takes around 18 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Devi Khadgamala Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Devi Khadgamala Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Supreme Sri Vidya prayer invoking all deities of Sri Chakra's nine enclosures.

seoFaqs.question: What does 'Khadgamala' literally mean and why is it called that?
seoFaqs.answer: Khadgamala is a compound of khadga (sword) and mala (garland), so the title means 'a garland of swords'. As Wikipedia and Hindupedia note, the stotram is said to bestow upon the reciter a garland of swords, where each sword represents the Devi's power to cut down a specific obstacle, principally desire (kama), hatred (krodha) and delusion (moha). The 'sword' is therefore an epithet for the Devi's discriminative power (jnana-khadga) that severs ignorance. Because the hymn names the deities one after another in sequence, the chain of names itself is visualised as a garland, and the cumulative effect of recitation is the donning of that garland of cutting wisdom around the chanter's neck.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Khadgamala structured around the Sri Chakra?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram functions as a guided mental tour through the nine avaranas (enclosures) of the Sri Chakra, beginning at the outermost square (Bhupura) and moving inward through each successive enclosure to the central bindu, where Lalita Tripura Sundari resides. As Wikipedia explains, the recitation pays homage to the yoginis and presiding deities at each power-point of the chakra, totalling around 98 named devis depending on recension. This structure makes the Khadgamala a complete Sri Chakra navarana puja in compressed verbal form. Sri Vidya practitioners use it as a substitute for full puja when materials or time are limited, since reciting the names is considered equivalent to mentally worshipping each devi at her station in the yantra.

seoFaqs.question: Which form of the Devi is the central deity of the Khadgamala?
seoFaqs.answer: The central deity is Lalita Tripura Sundari, the supreme Devi of the Sri Vidya tradition, also called Mahatripurasundari, Rajarajeshwari and Shri Mata. Hinduism Outlook and Hindupedia confirm that the inward journey of the stotram culminates at the bindu where Lalita is enthroned with her sugarcane bow, flowery arrows, noose and goad. The 98 surrounding yoginis are her avarana devatas, each governing one of the Sri Chakra's enclosures. Although peripheral devis include forms of Kali, Bhairavi and Bhuvaneshwari, the stotram's framing makes clear that all are emanations of Lalita herself, so reciting the Khadgamala is essentially a Lalita upasana expressed through her cosmic retinue.

seoFaqs.question: Does one need Sri Vidya initiation to recite the Khadgamala?
seoFaqs.answer: Strict Sri Vidya parampara holds that the full Khadgamala, with its accompanying nyasa, beeja seeds and Panchadashi mantra, is for initiates (diksha-pramanas) under a competent guru. However, in living householder practice, the stotram form (without the bija envelope) is widely published and chanted openly, as on Hinduism Outlook, Vedic Tithi and Bhaktinidhi. Most contemporary teachers distinguish between the upasaka path, which requires initiation, and the bhakta path of devotional recitation, which does not. Beginners are advised to recite the names with reverence, refrain from adding bija mantras until they receive diksha, and use the stotram as a daily Lalita parayana. On Fridays during Navratri it is especially recommended.

seoFaqs.question: What are the traditional benefits attributed to chanting the Khadgamala?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Vidya literature attributes a wide spectrum of fruits to consistent Khadgamala recitation, ranging from outer well-being (health, prosperity, harmonious relationships) to inner refinement (clarity of intellect, control of senses, dispassion). The signature benefit, encoded in the title, is the gift of jnana-khadga, the sword of discrimination that cuts through maya, kama, krodha and moha. As Hinduism Outlook and Hindupedia summarise, regular recitation is believed to align the chanter's subtle body with the Sri Chakra geometry, producing gradual purification of the chakras and integration of the mind. Devotees commonly undertake 41-day or maNDala parayana of the stotram with a specific sankalpa for spiritual progress.

seoFaqs.question: How is the Khadgamala different from the Lalita Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Both hymns name the Devi extensively, but they are structurally and functionally distinct. The Lalita Sahasranama, drawn from the Brahmanda Purana, lists 1000 names of Lalita in shloka form without geographical reference to the Sri Chakra, and its central rhythm is praise. The Khadgamala, by contrast, names roughly 98 deities arranged according to their precise position in the Sri Chakra avaranas, and is structured as a yantra-walking ritual rather than a pure praise-litany. As Wikipedia notes, recitation of Khadgamala is effectively a verbal navarana puja, while Lalita Sahasranama is praise-meditation. Many Sri Vidya sadhakas chant both daily, the Sahasranama for bhavana and the Khadgamala for yantra-installation.
